stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 investment portfolio options. The probability of favorable market conditions is uncertain, estimated between 41% and 61%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: bonds: $217K in good market conditions, $86K in bad market conditions. index fund: $89K in good market conditions, $24K in bad market conditions. tech stocks: $196K in good market conditions, $93K in bad market conditions. real estate: $287K in good market conditions, $90K in bad market conditions. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.51: best option is real estate with EV=$190.5K. real estate is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [170.8, 210.2].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
real estate is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
